_comment: Snapshot of the 'GitHub Deploy' Xcode Cloud workflow config, captured 2026-07-21. Xcode Cloud config lives entirely in App Store Connect, not in the repo -- this file plus recreate_xcode_cloud_workflow.py can recreate it via the App Store Connect API if it's ever deleted. Re-run the snapshot after any manual change in App Store Connect so this stays accurate.
name: GitHub Deploy
description: This workflow needs to exist for our GitHub workflow to be able to trigger a deploy with the API.
_comment_triggers: No automatic start conditions -- builds are triggered explicitly by .github/workflows/release-macos.yml via POST /v1/ciBuildRuns, not by Apple's native GitHub webhook (which never reliably fired for this repo). manualBranchStartCondition/manualTagStartCondition are a SEPARATE scoping mechanism from the automatic ones -- they control what's allowed for manual/API-triggered builds specifically. Both must allow-all, or POST /v1/ciBuildRuns with sourceBranchOrTag set to a tag fails with 409 ENTITY_ERROR.ATTRIBUTE.INVALID ('tag is not associated with the workflow') even though the tag is validly indexed.
_comment_relationships: IDs as of this snapshot -- re-fetch from the API if recreating the product/repository connection from scratch, these will change.
